steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank opens the second sentence. The first sentence states a limitation of Curiosity: it could not preserve samples for future analysis. The second sentence says NASA's team sought to build a mechanism that could do exactly that. The blank must signal that the second sentence is a result or response to the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Specifically,: Signals that what follows narrows or clarifies the previous point — but designing a new rover is a response, not a clarification. ✗
  • Choice B — Earlier,: Implies the second event happened before the first, which reverses the actual chronology. ✗
  • Choice C — Hence,: Signals consequence: the inability to preserve samples prompted NASA to build Perseverance with that capability. ✓
  • Choice D — Similarly,: Implies the two rovers share the same trait — but the passage is contrasting them. ✗
C is correct.
